text: We present NeuroVault (neurovault.org), an open-source, web-based repository designed for the storage, sharing, and interactive visualization of statistical maps of the human brain. While traditional publications often reduce complex 3D data to static 2D figures and limited tables of peak coordinates, NeuroVault allows researchers to upload and explore full unthresholded volumes. The platform utilizes modern web technologies to provide browser-based 2D and 3D visualization without the need for specialized software. By integrating with the Neurosynth database, NeuroVault enables real-time "cognitive decoding" to help researchers systematically interpret their findings. Furthermore, a public REST API ensures the data is programmatically accessible, fostering interoperability with other neuroinformatics tools. By facilitating the transition from coordinate-based to image-based meta-analysis, NeuroVault provides a critical resource for improving the transparency, longevity, and cumulative power of neuroimaging research.
